title: Distribution / Host Integration SSOT: Invariants

loop.observation: Distribution-host invariant details are generated from a focused sub-reader, but each detail manifest previously looked like independent loop debt.
loop.hypothesis: The invariants manifest can own the C11 invariant loop while detail manifests delegate to it.
loop.execute: Generate the invariants reader, load every detail fragment, verify hostintegration and store-distribution anchors, and publish invariant evidence.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale docs, missing fragments, malformed detail records, missing assertions, or missing source anchors.
loop.retrospective: C11 invariants stay one reviewable generated surface while detail manifests remain atomic semantic inputs.

summary: This SSOT fixes C11 host-integration invariants as generated documentation bound to `internal/hostintegration` executable models and store-distribution contracts.
The generated reader docs cover distribution channels, runtime roles, external provider CLI provenance, approved app data roots, and workspace grant materialization boundaries.

source_checks.name: helper-runtime-safety
source_checks.contains: store helper plan must not allow bundled provider CLIs

source_checks.name: external-tool-record
source_checks.contains: The executable path is local-only and must never be sent to C10.

source_checks.name: auto-detected-confirmation
source_checks.contains: auto-detected CLI should require confirmation in mac app store channel

source_checks.name: app-data-root-model
source_checks.contains: Store channels must not silently fall back to unmanaged home paths.

source_checks.name: mac-store-root-test
source_checks.contains: expected mac app store root without app group/container to fail

source_checks.name: msix-store-root-test
source_checks.contains: expected msix store root without package local data to fail

assertions: C11 owns distribution channel identity and host integration facts; provider capability remains C3 and runtime process execution remains C4.
Riido packages do not bundle provider CLIs; external provider tools enter C11 only as provenance-bearing local records.
Store App GUI adapters consume C11 contracts and may own OS entitlement calls, but must not spawn provider processes or copy C11 facts.
Store-managed channels require explicit consent and review-aware helper/runtime plans before background execution is allowed.
Approved app data roots and user workspace grants are separate concepts; C6 consumes only C11-approved roots and active grants.
Generated C11 invariants must stay source-checked against executable hostintegration models and store distribution contracts.
